data.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture justification). Scope: the gap-class remediation PLAN ("Close the Jira-sync producer↔consumer & tested-vs-shipped test-coverage gap class"), which cites this bug (3f04) and 0ee6/8b25/87e4 as motivating defects. Posting here as the discoverable anchor for that plan.

T1 fires: the plan crosses bright-lines (external integration = Jira REST/ACLI; a novel testing architecture = producer↔consumer snapshot-contract test fed by "hermetic-but-honest fixtures derived from real Jira payloads through the production code path").

AFFIRMATIONS (these pass T1 — positive feedback, no action needed):
- T1(a)/(b): The Context does substantial, well-reasoned external prior-art work — Pact (consumer-driven contracts), vcrpy semantic matchers, Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel structure-not-value validation, the Google SWE-book "verified fakes" + change-detector warning, and a concrete read of how real Jira clients test (jira-python live Dockerized/Cloud sandbox vs. jira-cli on-disk fixtures captured from real Jira through production serialization via an in-process mock). It then JUSTIFIES the chosen point on that spectrum, explicitly rejecting full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact as "overkill for a single team." This is exactly the anti-repackaging / established-approach-comparison T1 wants.
- T1(b) Rule-of-Three: three same-class production escapes (0ee6, 3f04, plus the comment-bug pattern) supply a genuine recurrence justification for the new contract-test pattern rather than speculative novelty.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] [T1(a)] The plan surveys EXTERNAL prior art thoroughly but omits the IN-REPO prior art that most directly overlaps its chosen approach: tests/mocks/jira-cassette-loader.py — a VCR-style cassette replay loader built from a prior "SDET audit P3-2" whose stated verdict is "VCR-style cassette replay against recorded [Jira] interactions is the recommended pattern." That is precisely the plan's "hermetic-but-honest fixtures derived from real Jira payloads, exercised through the production path." The plan should reconcile with it: extend/adopt the cassette loader as the fixture substrate, OR explicitly retire it (it is currently orphaned — `search_files cassette` shows it is referenced only by its own self-test) so the team does not end up with two parallel hermetic-Jira-fixture mechanisms. AC2 ("fixtures derived from real Jira payloads") and the "no net increase in brittle tests" AC both turn on this decision.
  evidence: tests/mocks/jira-cassette-loader.py:2-6,27-29 (SDET audit P3-2, "SCAFFOLD only ... Live recording ... is a multi-day follow-up"); `search_files "cassette"` returns hits only within that file; plan Context lists Pact/vcrpy/Schemathesis/Dredd/SWE-book/jira-python/jira-cli but never the in-repo loader.

- [ ] [T1(c)] Resolve the unverified external-capability assertions before committing. The Context leans on specific tool capabilities as design influences — "vcrpy semantic matchers" and "Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel structure-not-value validation" — but the plan never states whether ANY of these become an actual dependency or are inspiration only. The repo's existing cassette scaffold is deliberately "Stdlib only — no vcr.py or responses package dependency" (jira-cassette-loader.py:23). The plan should state explicitly: which (if any) of these libraries are adopted, and if none, that the contract/structure-validation is implemented in-house — so an unverified "library supports X" claim isn't load-bearing for the new architecture.
  evidence: plan Context ("vcrpy semantic matchers; Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel structure-not-value validation"); jira-cassette-loader.py:23 ("Stdlib only — no vcr.py or responses package dependency"); plan AC6 mandates "assert semantic round-trip + structure/type" without naming an implementation vehicle.

- [ ] [T1(a)] Reconcile the proposed "single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema" (AC1) with the EXISTING in-repo schema machinery so it extends rather than reinvents: invariants.report_schema_drift(issue_key, observed, expected) (invariants.py:304) and health.SCHEMA_VERSION already encode a drift-detection concept for Jira shapes. The plan should state whether the new producer↔consumer schema subsumes, feeds, or supersedes report_schema_drift, so the codebase ends with one schema-of-record, not two overlapping drift mechanisms.
  evidence: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/invariants.py:304 (`def report_schema_drift`); health.py:59 (`SCHEMA_VERSION`); plan AC1 ("single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema").

Note (not a T1 finding, flagged for the contract-test pass): tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py:75 documents that ACLI emits `{issueLinks:[{typeName,outwardIssueKey}]}` while the differs read REST-shape `issuelinks` — so "real Jira payloads" fixtures must pin WHICH serialization they capture. Raising under the round-trip/contract criterion, not T1.
